hook: Brand-new or resale landed — which side is blinking first in April 2026?

slides.sub: Developers are holding ask. Resale sellers are moving. Here's what the week's caveats show.

slides.lead: Two markets, two postures — and the gap is widening this week.

headline_parts.text: initial ask — while new launches hold

slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy analysis · Apr 2026

slides.pre: Avg resale landed: discount to first listing
slides.post: Across 18 resale transactions tracked this week — widest gap seen since Q3 2023.

slides.title: Three forces behind the resale softness

items.body: ABSD recalibration in Feb 2026 thinned the upgrader pool — fewer Singaporeans moving from condo to landed in one step.
New launches in D19 (Seletar / Yio Chu Kang) priced at S$1,600–1,750 psf are anchoring expectations, squeezing resale sellers in the same belt.
Sellers who bought in 2021–2022 at peak are now the most willing to cut — their equity cushion still allows a 7–10% concession.

slides.source: URA · HDB resale data · MAS ABSD records · 2026

slides.title: Districts by resale ask-to-close gap

items.label: District 10
items.sub: Bukit Timah — thinnest discount, still moving

slides.source: URA caveats · SRX landed · POV Guy calc · Apr 2026

slides.label: The real signal
slides.punch: Developers can afford to hold ask because their land cost is locked. Resale sellers carry opportunity cost every month they wait — and the patient buyer knows it.
slides.punch_bold: The week's data says negotiate hard on resale, move fast on new if the location fits.

slides.title: What brand-new landed is printing this week

items.body: D19 new terrace launches transacting at S$1,680–1,750 psf — held firm, zero reported discount to indicative list.
D26 new semi-Ds moving at S$1,590–1,640 psf; developer releasing units in tranches to defend price integrity.
Implied premium vs comparable resale stock in same districts: S$180–260 psf — yet buyers are paying it for fresh tenure and no renovation overhang.

slides.source: Developer price lists · URA caveats · Apr 2026

slides.pre: Weighing landed in D10 / D19 / D26?

slides.sub: If you know anyone deciding between new launch and resale landed right now, send them this.

caption:
Brand-new landed is holding firm. Resale is blinking. Here's the gap. 🏠

• Resale landed closed ~7% below first ask this week across 18 tracked transactions
• New launches in D19 and D26 held list price — zero reported developer concessions
• The ask-to-close gap is widest in D26 (–9.1%) and narrowest in D10 (–4.2%)

If you're buying landed in 2026, the side you choose changes your negotiation entirely — and this week's data makes that very clear.
